topic: Machine Learning: Classification vs Regression Scenarios

questions.scenario: A real-world industrial problem involves predicting the likelihood of a customer buying a car based on their age, income, and credit score. The dataset is highly skewed, with only 10% of customers purchasing a car. Which machine learning algorithm is most suitable for handling this imbalance and providing accurate predictions?
questions.question: Which algorithm should be chosen for this task?
options: A) Logistic Regression with oversampling of minority class
B) Decision Trees with cost-sensitive learning
C) Random Forest with undersampling of majority class
D) Support Vector Machines with class weights
questions.correct_answer_text: D) Support Vector Machines with class weights
questions.explanation: Support Vector Machines with class weights can handle class imbalance by assigning different weights to different classes, allowing for more accurate predictions on the minority class.

questions.scenario: In a high-dimensional space, a company wants to predict house prices based on features like the number of bedrooms, square footage, location, and amenities. However, the dataset contains many redundant and correlated features. Which dimensionality reduction technique is most suitable for this scenario?
questions.question: Which technique should be used to reduce dimensionality?
options: A) Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for feature extraction
B) t-Distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE) for visualization
C) Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA) for feature selection
D) Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE) for feature ranking
questions.correct_answer_text: A) Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for feature extraction
questions.explanation: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is a suitable technique for reducing dimensionality in high-dimensional spaces by extracting the most important features that capture the majority of the variance in the data.

questions.scenario: A real-time system requires classification of network packets as either legitimate or malicious in less than 10 milliseconds. The system has limited computational resources and must handle a high volume of packets. Which machine learning algorithm is most suitable for this real-time constraint and limited resources?
questions.question: Which algorithm should be chosen for real-time classification?
options: A) Deep Neural Networks with early exit
B) Gradient Boosting Machines with tree pruning
C) K-Nearest Neighbors with approximate search
D) Decision Trees with caching and parallelization
questions.correct_answer_text: D) Decision Trees with caching and parallelization
questions.explanation: Decision Trees with caching and parallelization can provide fast and efficient classification in real-time systems with limited resources by reducing the number of computations and utilizing parallel processing.
